channel.title: morningNewsSection1
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from morningNewsSection1, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Opinion: The enduring bravery of Captain Sully
item.description: Retired pilot Chesley "Sully" Sullenberger, famous for landing a plane on the Hudson River in 2009, recently revealed he has Alzheimer's disease.

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Finding World Cup joy in talking with women who love soccer
item.description: World Cup coverage often shows male fans, but a highlight of this tournament has been speaking with passionate female soccer enthusiasts.

item.title: Why the U.S. struggles to achieve victory in wars
item.description: U.S. presidents have promised quick, decisive wars in Afghanistan, Iraq, and Iran, but all have proven far more challenging than anticipated.

item.title: Two U.S. service members killed, one missing after Iranian attack in Jordan
item.description: Two U.S. service members died while partner forces defended against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks, according to U.S. Central Command.
